sentence: Usted sabe que el cielo está claro esta mañana.
ipa: uˈsteð ˈsaβe ke el ˈsjelo eˈsta ˈklaɾo ˈesta maˈɲana
gloss_en: You know the sky is clear this morning.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. Costa Rican ustedeo (sabe) per penny2002.

sentence: Diay mae, tráigase un café y venga rápido.
ipa: ˈdjai ˈmae ˈtɾaiɣase un kaˈfe i ˈbenɡa ˈrapiðo
gloss_en: Hey buddy, grab a coffee and come quick.
notes: strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. Costa Rican 'mae' and 'diay' per penny2002.

sentence: El güila tiene un perro grande y juguetón.
ipa: el ˈɡwila ˈtjene un ˈpero ˈɡɾande i xuɡeˈton
gloss_en: The kid has a big, playful dog.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. Costa Rican 'güila' for a child per penny2002.

sentence: Mañana la niña juega cerca de la plaza.
ipa: maˈɲana la ˈniɲa ˈxweɣa ˈseɾka de la ˈplasa
gloss_en: Tomorrow the girl plays near the square.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we] per hualde2005. general Costa Rican usage per penny2002.

sentence: La calle está llena de gente joven.
ipa: la ˈkaʝe eˈsta ˈʝena de ˈxente ˈxoben
gloss_en: The street is full of young people.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Quiere arroz con pollo o prefiere gallo pinto?
ipa: ˈkjeɾe aˈros kon ˈpoʝo o pɾeˈfjeɾe ˈɡaʝo ˈpinto
gloss_en: Do you want rice with chicken or gallo pinto?
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. ustedeo (quiere); Costa Rican 'gallo pinto' per penny2002.

sentence: Busque un zapato nuevo en el centro.
ipa: ˈbuske un saˈpato ˈnwebo en el ˈsentɾo
gloss_en: Look for a new shoe downtown.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. ustedeo imperative (busque) per penny2002.

sentence: La lluvia cae fuerte sobre el techo viejo.
ipa: la ˈʝubja ˈkae ˈfweɾte ˈsobɾe el ˈtetʃo ˈbjexo
gloss_en: The rain falls hard on the old roof.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: El vecino dice que quiere vender la finca.
ipa: el beˈsino ˈdise ke ˈkjeɾe beˈndeɾ la ˈfinka
gloss_en: The neighbor says he wants to sell the farm.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we] per hualde2005. seseo on 'vecino', 'dice' per penny2002.

sentence: El carro rojo corre rápido por la carretera.
ipa: el ˈkaro ˈroxo ˈkore ˈrapiðo poɾ la kareˈteɾa
gloss_en: The red car runs fast along the highway.
notes: strong-R trill [r] per hualde2005. assibilated trill and conservative [s], per Costa Rican norm per penny2002.

sentence: Ese señor tiene doce vacas en la montaña.
ipa: ˈese seˈɲoɾ ˈtjene ˈdose ˈbakas en la moˈntaɲa
gloss_en: That man has twelve cows on the mountain.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: No vaya solo, ande con su compañero.
ipa: ˈno baˈʝa ˈsolo ˈande kon su kompaˈɲeɾo
gloss_en: Don't go alone, go with your companion.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. ustedeo negative imperative (vaya, ande) per penny2002.

sentence: Todo bien, pura vida, muchas gracias.
ipa: ˈtoðo ˈbjen ˈpuɾa ˈbiða ˈmutʃas ˈɡɾasjas
gloss_en: All good, pura vida, thank you very much.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. Costa Rican 'pura vida' per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Vive usted cerca del parque nuevo?
ipa: ˈbibe uˈsteð ˈseɾka del ˈpaɾke ˈnwebo
gloss_en: Do you live near the new park?
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. ustedeo (vive usted) per penny2002.

sentence: El caballo blanco pasta junto al río.
ipa: el kaˈβaʝo ˈblanko ˈpasta ˈxunto al ˈrio
gloss_en: The white horse grazes by the river.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: Compre cinco panes y guarde un poco.
ipa: ˈkompɾe ˈsinko ˈpanes i ˈɡwaɾde un ˈpoko
gloss_en: Buy five loaves and save some.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. ustedeo imperatives (compre, guarde) per penny2002.

sentence: Los zapatos viejos ya no sirven para nada.
ipa: los saˈpatos ˈbjexos ˈʝa ˈno ˈsiɾben paɾa ˈnaða
gloss_en: The old shoes are no good for anything anymore.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: Aquella niña baila con mucha alegría.
ipa: aˈkeʝa ˈniɲa ˈbajla kon ˈmutʃa aleˈɡɾia
gloss_en: That girl dances with great joy.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.

sentence: Tiene que llamar a su mamá temprano.
ipa: ˈtjene ke ʝaˈmaɾ a su maˈma teˈmpɾano
gloss_en: You have to call your mom early.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we] per hualde2005. ustedeo (tiene) per penny2002.

sentence: El sol brilla sobre el mar azul y tranquilo.
ipa: el ˈsol ˈbɾiʝa ˈsobɾe el ˈmaɾ aˈsul i tɾaˈnkilo
gloss_en: The sun shines over the blue, calm sea.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per hualde2005. general usage per penny2002.
